title: crun on Fedora 31 cannot start systemd-based containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Upgraded Fedora 31 and using crun, cannot start systemd-based containers
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Upgrade to Fedora 31. Switch runtime to crun

2. Run a systemd based container: `podman run --rm --entrypoint /sbin/init centos:7`


3.

**Describe the results you received:**
The podman command stalls and ends with timeout error

```
DEBU[0001] Created OCI spec for container 6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f at /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
-containers/6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f/userdata/config.json 
DEBU[0001] /usr/bin/conmon messages will be logged to syslog 
DEBU[0001] running conmon: /usr/bin/conmon               args="[--api-version 1 -s -c 6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2
b9f8f -u 6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f -r /usr/bin/crun -b /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6572
a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f/userdata -p /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6572a8109e48120addb5bbb91
9e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f/userdata/pidfile -l k8s-file:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76
b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f/userdata/ctr.log --exit-dir /var/run/libpod/exits --socket-dir-path /var/run/libpod/socket --log-level debug 
--syslog -t --conmon-pidfile /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f/user
data/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/lib/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runr
oot --exit-command-arg /var/run/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --
exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /var/run/libpod --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --ex
it-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on

--exit-command-arg 6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f]"
INFO[0001] Running conmon under slice machine.slice and unitName libpod-conmon-6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f.s
cope

DEBU[0241] Tearing down network namespace at /var/run/netns/cni-b4cdc70a-b007-e05d-21aa-1c054cf3dc99 for container 6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b

INFO[0241] Got pod network &{Name:blissful_ramanujan Namespace:blissful_ramanujan ID:6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b

nges:[]}]} 
INFO[0241] About to del CNI network podman (type=bridge) 
DEBU[0241] unmounted container "6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f" 
DEBU[0243] unable to remove container 6572a8109e48120addb5bbb919e76b6ab949408a95d6cfe07ace3e71aa2b9f8f after failing to start and attach to it

ERRO[0243] container creation timeout: internal libpod error 

```


**Describe the results you expected:**
container starts

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

1. Issue remains with podman 1.6.2-2 from koji
1. Non-systemd (e.g. --entrypoint /bin/bash) works

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

comments.body:
The podman command stalls for a few minutes before the timeout error.

With the non-systemd entrypoint I get

```
 DEBU[0003] Received: 24102                              
INFO[0003] Got Conmon PID as 24095                      
DEBU[0003] Created container 7d78e209ad40a792441260aa69799e856edd0a71187c156f76fc240183e00d85 in OCI runtime 
DEBU[0004] Attaching to container 7d78e209ad40a792441260aa69799e856edd0a71187c156f76fc240183e00d85 
DEBU[0004] connecting to socket /var/run/libpod/socket/7d78e209ad40a792441260aa69799e856edd0a71187c156f76fc240183e00d85/attach 
DEBU[0004] Received a resize event: {Width:145 Height:49} 
DEBU[0004] Starting container 7d78e209ad40a792441260aa69799e856edd0a71187c156f76fc240183e00d85 with command [/bin/bash] 
DEBU[0004] Started container 7d78e209ad40a792441260aa69799e856edd0a71187c156f76fc240183e00d85 
DEBU[0004] Enabling signal proxying                     
```

With `/sbin/init` as entrypoint it stalls at `conmon` before forking `crun`:


```
INFO[0002] Running conmon under slice machine.slice and unitName
libpod-conmon-fb8b7387c837e9c797982929dc9c3eb316bd5e7454729017c86c5224c1107a87.scope 

# journal
systemdhook <error>: f4a19f6dec13: Failed to get memory subsystem path for the process: Success
conmon f4a19f6dec13ae64449f <error>: Failed to create container: exit status 255
```

closing as duplicate of: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4226

Shortly, the systemd version inside of the container doesn't understand cgroups v2

@giuseppe  This is a different problem: this is not the same as 4226.

This is caused by an F30 -> F31 upgrade having

oci-register-machine-0-11.git66fa845.fc31.x86_64
oci-systemd-hook-0.2.0-2.git05e6923.fc31.x86_64
oci-umount-2.5-3.gitc3cda1f.fc31.x86_64


These packages are not installed by default in a clean F31 enviornment.

It seems that these packages prevent proper functioning of podman with systemd-based containers that are cgroupv2 aware.

With these package removed fedora:31 and centos:8 systemd containers can now run.

I have `retired` oci-register-machine and oci-systemd-hook from the Fedora (31, 32, Rawhide) distribution.  This means these packages will no longer be available to be installed.  Sadly this will not remove them from already installed systems.  

I have asked for podman to Obsolete: oci-systemd-hook, which will remove an oci-systemd-hook package on upgrade.

Since the oci-systemd-hook is the issue this should fix your problem.  Also conmon package has a fix in conmon-2.0.2-1.fc31.x86_64, that will ignore hook errors.  The oci-systemd-hook package is returning an error, which is causing everything to fail.  If you update to this package, I believe systemd would work within the container.

since I just ended up on this issue after debugging an issue with running a s6 based container (so `/ìnit`) on podman 3.0.1 on Fedora 33, that always failed with:

```
DEBU[0000] ExitCode msg: "error executing hook `/usr/libexec/oci/hooks.d/oci-systemd-hook` (exit code: 1): oci runtime error" 
Error: OCI runtime error: error executing hook `/usr/libexec/oci/hooks.d/oci-systemd-hook` (exit code: 1)
```

even when started with `--systemd=false` or as root.

Removing the oci-systemd-hook package fixed the issue. Would have been great if that package was removed on a upgrade.
@lsm5 Is there a reason we still maintain `oci-systemd-hook`? Can we deprecate + remove the package?
At the very least, we probably want to make it conflict with Podman

We can do that moving forward, but no way we are going back to F31.  :^)
@lsm5 PTAL

@duritong Thank you! We had the same issue on a developer's system (and same fix), but the `podman` error message was different:
```
Error: OCI runtime error: unable to start container f3c6c6da53ebb84894d21de4cbeb6c48f3d7011cd746860bc6cf29c9d9fb5d80: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: error running hook #1: error running hook: exit status 1, stdout: , stderr:
exit code: 125
```
`journalctl` showed:
```
oci-systemd-hook[160629]: systemdhook <error>: f3c6c6da53eb: Failed to get memory subsystem path for the process: File exists
```
`oci-systemd-hook` should not be needed anymore with Podman.  Could you uninstall the `oci-systemd-hook` package?
oci-systemd-hook has already been removed from Fedora. @mikedep333 @duritong did you see this on an active Fedora version?

I think what happened is that `oci-systemd-hook` was pulled in an earlier version as a dependency, then got dropped as a dependency, but stayed installed on system that had podman before. And thus you had to uninstall it manually.

Afaik there is no way to clean up packages automatically on upgrades, but this is an example that gives you an example, where it would be handy.

> I think what happened is that `oci-systemd-hook` was pulled in an earlier version as a dependency, then got dropped as a dependency, but stayed installed on system that had podman before. And thus you had to uninstall it manually.
> 
> Afaik there is no way to clean up packages automatically on upgrades, but this is an example that gives you an example, where it would be handy.

I guess `dnf autoremove` could have helped, not sure if it's possible to automate the autoremove option in dnf config though.
